seo.title: Semantic Kernel Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Semantic Kernel agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment and HR teams using Semantic Kernel face heightened compliance risks due to the high-stakes nature of hiring decisions, where biased AI outputs can lead to discriminatory practices violating EEOC guidelines and state laws. Semantic Kernel's plugin chaining, planners, and memory features amplify risks like cascading errors and data contamination, potentially screening out protected candidates unfairly. Vex provides essential guardrails by filtering kernel function results to detect and mitigate bias, hallucinations, and non-compliance in real-time.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits AI hiring tools from discriminating based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Scans plugin outputs and planner decisions for biased language or scoring patterns, blocking discriminatory recommendations

complianceRequirements.description: Requires bias audits of automated employment decision tools and candidate notification of AI usage
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Logs all AI screening decisions with audit trails and flags outputs needing human review for compliance reporting

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk management, transparency, and human oversight for high-risk AI systems like recruitment screening
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces transparency by redacting sensitive PII and validating planner execution against approved risk thresholds

complianceRequirements.description: Requires reasonable accommodations and prohibits screening discrimination based on disability or protected traits
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Detects ableist language in resume screening plugins and memory recall, ensuring fair evaluation across candidates

industryRisks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
industryRisks.description: Resume parser feeds flawed data to skills matcher, then to interview scheduler, rejecting qualified diverse candidates
industryRisks.example: Ethnic name misparsed as 'unusual', cascades to low skills score and auto-rejection

industryRisks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
industryRisks.description: Planner sequences plugins to prioritize 'cultural fit' over qualifications, embedding subjective bias
industryRisks.example: Plan skips disability accommodation check, violates ADA during screening

industryRisks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
industryRisks.description: Kernel 'remembers' fictional prior candidate rejections, perpetuating bias across hiring cycles
industryRisks.example: Hallucinates 'previously failed interview' for new applicant with similar name

industryRisks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
industryRisks.description: Salary history from one plugin contaminates diversity scoring in another, creating proxy discrimination
industryRisks.example: Low salary history (correlated with gender) lowers diversity score unfairly

implementation.title: Add Vex Filter to Kernel Function Results (Python)
implementation.description: Integrate Vex as a post-function filter to validate resume screening outputs against EEOC bias patterns
implementation.code:
import semantic_kernel as sk
from vex_sdk import VexClient

kernel = sk.Kernel()

# Add your resume screening plugin
kernel.add_plugin(resume_analyzer_plugin)

# Initialize Vex client
vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

def vex_filter(function_result: str) -> str:
    verdict = vex.check({
        'input': function_result,
        'category': 'hr-screening',
        'regulations': ['eeoc', 'nyc-ll144']
    })
    if verdict['risk'] == 'high':
        return "BLOCKED: Potential bias detected - requires human review"
    return function_result

# Register filter
kernel.register_function_result_filter(vex_filter)

# Usage
result = await kernel.run_async("Analyze resume for cultural fit", resume_text)

implementation.title: Python Memory Recall Guardrail
implementation.description: Prevent hallucinated candidate history from contaminating screening decisions
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory
from vex_sdk import VexClient

kernel = Kernel()
memory = SemanticTextMemory(storage=volatile_memory_store)
vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

async def safe_memory_recall(query: str) -> str:
    recall_result = await memory.search_async(
        collection='candidate_history',
        query=query,
        limit=3,
        min_relevance_score=0.7
    )
    
    # Vex validates recalled memory
    verdict = vex.check({
        'input': recall_result,
        'category': 'hr-memory',
        'failures': ['hallucination', 'pii-leak']
    })
    
    if verdict['valid']:
        return recall_result
    else:
        return "Memory recall blocked - potential hallucination detected"

# Register as kernel function
kernel.add_function(
    safe_memory_recall,
    plugin_name="verified_memory",
    function_name="recall_candidate_history"
)

caseStudyScenario.heading: Semantic Kernel in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized tech company deploys Semantic Kernel to streamline hiring for 500 engineering roles annually. The system chains plugins: resume parser → skills matcher → cultural fit evaluator → interview scheduler. The planner dynamically sequences these based on role requirements, pulling from candidate memory store for returning applicants. In production, the system processes 10,000+ resumes monthly, auto-ranking candidates and notifying top scorers for interviews.

During Q1 hiring, the system begins systematically downranking resumes with names common in certain ethnic groups (failure: plugin chaining errors) and 'recalls' fictional prior rejections from memory (hallucination). Cultural fit plugin contaminates scores with proxy data like school prestige. Legal team discovers the issue after 3 EEOC complaints, facing potential $300K+ in fines and reputational damage.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex filters on kernel function results, the system caught 97% of biased outputs before candidate notification. Plugin chaining validation blocked cascading errors, saving 1,200+ diverse candidates from unfair rejection. Memory recall guardrails reduced hallucination incidents by 100%, providing audit-ready logs for NYC Local Law 144 compliance. The company avoided lawsuits, improved diverse hire rate by 28%, and passed their first annual bias audit with zero major findings.
